messages.content:
Clarify the role of 'login.cgi' in this vulnerability scenario.

A cross-site scripting (xss) vulnerability exists in the login.cgi set_lang_CountryCode() functionality of Wavlink AC3000 M33A8.V5030.210505. A specially crafted HTTP request can lead to a disclosure of sensitive information. An attacker can make an unauthenticated HTTP request to trigger this vulnerability.

The 'login.cgi' script is involved as it contains the function 'set_lang_CountryCode()' where the XSS vulnerability resides. Exploiting this function through a crafted request allows attackers to execute scripts, leading to sensitive information exposure.
